title: MIMIC Retrospective Validation Run — April 2026

dataset.name: MIMIC-IV de-identified retrospective validation subset
dataset.data_status: De-identified retrospective data. Public outputs are aggregate and DUA-sanitized.
dataset.event_definition_note: Latest real-engine run reports clustered event episodes, not raw event rows.

headline: ERA maintained a stable 4.0-hour median lead time across configurable thresholds while trading detection sensitivity for lower alert burden.

default_threshold.framing: Most selective; optimized for low false positives and alert-burden reduction.

threshold_profiles.framing: Higher detection, more alerts; useful when higher sensitivity is preferred.
Most conservative; optimized for low false positives and alert-burden reduction.

next_validation_metrics.status: Updated from latest DUA-safe real-engine retrospective validation run.
next_validation_metrics.note: These metrics require row-level timestamps, event indicators, patient IDs, and alert flags or risk scores.

score_saturation_display_plan.problem: Peak risk values can cluster at the upper cap, which may make high-risk patients look too similar.
presentation_fix: Priority Tier: Low / Watch / Elevated / Critical
Primary Driver: SpO2 decline / HR instability / BP trend / RR elevation
First Crossed Threshold: hours before current review
score_saturation_display_plan.principle: Do not rely on raw score alone. Foreground prioritization, trend, reason codes, and review context.

approved_claim: Retrospective analysis on de-identified MIMIC data showed ERA can support configurable review-prioritization workflows with substantially reduced alert burden and stable retrospective lead-time context.
decision_support_notice: Decision support only. Not intended to diagnose, direct treatment, replace clinician judgment, or independently trigger escalation.

lead_time_before_event.important_note: This CSV did not contain era_alert or risk_score columns, so the script used a transparent vitals-based proxy score.
lead_time_before_event.headline: Among detected event clusters, ERA flags appeared a median of 4.0 hours before documented event timestamps within the 6-hour pre-event window.
lead_time_before_event.clinical_note: Lead time is calculated from the first ERA alert inside the configured pre-event window to the documented event timestamp. This is retrospective, de-identified analysis and requires independent clinical review.

lead_time_headline: Across t=4.0, t=5.0, and t=6.0, median lead time remained 4.0 hours among detected event clusters.
lead_time_note: This CSV did not contain era_alert or risk_score columns, so the script used a transparent vitals-based proxy score.

explainability_display_upgrade.purpose: Reduce score saturation concerns by foregrounding clinical prioritization context instead of raw score alone.

operational_alert_burden_comparison.status: Added for Validation Intelligence Polish V2
operational_alert_burden_comparison.method_note: Standard threshold-only alerts per patient-day is approximated from standard threshold total alerts divided by total patient-days from the row-level retrospective lead-time analysis.
operational_alert_burden_comparison.pilot_safe_summary: ERA generated approximately 5.7468 alerts per patient-day versus approximately 177.2131 alerts per patient-day under standard threshold-only alerting, for an alert reduction of 96.8%.

polished_validation_summary_v2.purpose: Make Validation Intelligence read like a hospital-facing validation showcase instead of internal developer notes.
polished_validation_summary_v2.hero_statement: Retrospective analysis on de-identified MIMIC data showed ERA can reduce alert burden while maintaining configurable patient-level detection in a 6-hour pre-event window.
polished_validation_summary_v2.lead_time_plain_language: Among detected event clusters, the median time from first ERA flag to documented event timestamp was 4.0 hours within the 6-hour pre-event window.
polished_validation_summary_v2.driver_note: Composite multi-signal pattern rows are cases where multiple monitored signals contributed to prioritization without one single dominant driver.
display_priority: 4.0-hour median first-flag timing among detected event clusters
Threshold profiles for ICU, mixed units, and telemetry/stepdown
Representative review examples with lead time and primary driver

pilot_evidence_export.purpose: Printable and downloadable validation summary for hospitals, investors, advisors, and pilot review.

validation_showcase_cleanup_v3.purpose: Make Validation Intelligence and Pilot Evidence pages read as polished hospital-facing validation assets instead of internal notes.
visible_improvements: 81% alert reduction becomes dominant hero metric
Driver breakdown collapsed behind review toggle
Download JSON and representative examples CSV preserved
validation_showcase_cleanup_v3.lead_time_safe_framing: Among detected event clusters, the median time from first ERA flag to documented event timestamp was approximately 4.0 hours within the 6-hour pre-event window.
validation_showcase_cleanup_v3.pilot_safe_headline: Retrospective analysis on de-identified MIMIC data showed ERA can reduce alert burden while maintaining configurable patient-level detection in a 6-hour pre-event window.

mimic_dua_public_safety.public_policy: Only aggregate metrics, sanitized case examples, validation methodology, and code are public-facing. Row-level MIMIC-derived exports remain local-only.
mimic_dua_public_safety.notice: Decision support only. Retrospective analysis only. Not intended to diagnose, direct treatment, replace clinician judgment, or independently trigger escalation.

real_engine_dua_safe_public_wording_v2.source: Latest DUA-safe real-engine retrospective validation run summary
real_engine_dua_safe_public_wording_v2.hero_statement: At conservative threshold t=6.0, ERA generated approximately 94.3% fewer alerts than standard threshold-only alerting while maintaining a low 4.2% false-positive rate.
real_engine_dua_safe_public_wording_v2.lead_time_statement: Among detected event clusters, the median time from first ERA flag to documented event timestamp was approximately 4.0 hours within the 6-hour pre-event window.
real_engine_dua_safe_public_wording_v2.operational_burden_statement: ERA generated approximately 0.6467 alerts per patient-day versus approximately 11.2743 alerts per patient-day under standard threshold-only alerting.

threshold_story.t4: High-acuity / ICU review mode: higher detection with more alerts, while still reducing alert burden versus standard threshold-only alerting.
threshold_story.t5: Balanced review mode: intermediate detection and alert burden.
threshold_story.t6: Conservative telemetry / stepdown review mode: intentionally selective, optimized for alert-burden reduction and low false positives.

real_engine_dua_safe_public_wording_v2.public_framing: Retrospective analysis on de-identified MIMIC data showed ERA can support configurable review-prioritization workflows with substantially reduced alert burden and retrospective lead-time context.
avoid_overclaim: Do not say ERA predicts deterioration.
Do not say ERA prevents adverse events.
Do not say ERA replaces standard monitoring.
Do not say ERA outperforms clinicians.
Do not describe retrospective lead time as prospective proof.

duasafe_threshold_matrix.purpose: DUA-safe aggregate threshold matrix for comparing selected ERA operating points.
duasafe_threshold_matrix.public_policy: Aggregate metrics only. No row-level MIMIC data, no MIMIC IDs, and no exact patient-linked timestamps.

threshold_matrix.framing: Higher detection, more alerts; useful when higher sensitivity is preferred.
Most conservative; optimized for low false positives and alert-burden reduction.

recommended_story.t4: t=4.0 is the high-acuity / ICU operating point with higher detection and more alerts.
recommended_story.t5: t=5.0 is the balanced mixed-unit operating point.
recommended_story.t6: t=6.0 is the conservative telemetry / stepdown operating point optimized for low alert burden and low false positives.

duasafe_threshold_matrix.pilot_safe_claim: Retrospective analysis on de-identified MIMIC data showed ERA can support configurable review-prioritization workflows with substantially reduced alert burden and retrospective lead-time context.
duasafe_threshold_matrix.notice: Decision support only. Retrospective analysis only. Not intended to diagnose, direct treatment, replace clinician judgment, or independently trigger escalation.

real_engine_threshold_matrix_status: DUA-safe selected-threshold matrix completed for t=4.0, t=5.0, and t=6.0.

threshold_matrix_public_story_v1.primary_finding: Median lead time remained 4.0 hours across t=4.0, t=5.0, and t=6.0.
threshold_matrix_public_story_v1.public_headline: ERA maintained a stable 4.0-hour median lead time across configurable thresholds while trading detection sensitivity for lower alert burden.

operating_point_story.t4: ICU / high-acuity mode: higher detection with more alerts.
operating_point_story.t6: Telemetry / stepdown conservative mode: most selective, lowest alert burden, lowest FPR.

threshold_matrix_public_story_v1.operational_burden_highlight: At t=6.0, ERA generated approximately 0.6467 alerts per patient-day versus approximately 11.2743 alerts per patient-day under standard threshold-only alerting.
threshold_matrix_public_story_v1.dua_policy: Public outputs use aggregate metrics and sanitized Case-001 style examples only. Row-level MIMIC-derived exports remain local-only.

lead_time_window_sensitivity.purpose: DUA-safe aggregate lead-time robustness and event-window sensitivity testing.
lead_time_window_sensitivity.public_policy: Aggregate metrics only. No row-level MIMIC data, no MIMIC IDs, no exact patient-linked timestamps.
lead_time_window_sensitivity.pilot_safe_claim: Retrospective analysis on de-identified MIMIC data showed ERA can support configurable review-prioritization workflows with substantially reduced alert burden and retrospective lead-time context.
lead_time_window_sensitivity.notice: Decision support only. Retrospective analysis only. Not intended to diagnose, direct treatment, replace clinician judgment, or independently trigger escalation.

interpretation_guidance.main_question: Does lead time remain meaningful when event-window assumptions change?
interpretation_guidance.hospital_review_use: Supports discussion of whether timing behavior is robust across 3-hour, 6-hour, and 12-hour retrospective windows.
interpretation_guidance.caution: Retrospective timing behavior should not be described as prospective proof.

public_story.primary_finding: Lead-time behavior scaled predictably across 3-hour, 6-hour, and 12-hour retrospective event windows.
public_story.stability_finding: Alert reduction, FPR, and alerts-per-patient-day remained stable for each threshold because those values are tied to operating point behavior rather than event-window size.
public_story.pilot_safe_interpretation: This robustness matrix supports a controlled pilot conversation by showing that ERA’s alert-burden behavior remains stable while detection and retrospective lead-time scale with the size of the pre-event review window.
public_story.caution: Retrospective timing behavior should not be described as prospective proof.

lead_time_robustness_public_story_v1.primary_finding: Lead-time behavior scaled predictably across 3-hour, 6-hour, and 12-hour retrospective event windows.
lead_time_robustness_public_story_v1.stability_finding: Alert reduction, FPR, and alerts-per-patient-day remained stable for each threshold because those values are tied to operating point behavior rather than event-window size.
lead_time_robustness_public_story_v1.pilot_safe_interpretation: This robustness matrix supports a controlled pilot conversation by showing that ERA’s alert-burden behavior remains stable while detection and retrospective lead-time scale with the size of the pre-event review window.
lead_time_robustness_public_story_v1.caution: Retrospective timing behavior should not be described as prospective proof.

latest_progress_update.summary: DUA-safe aggregate threshold matrix completed on local-only ERA-format eICU cohort.
latest_progress_update.public_takeaway: MIMIC-IV evidence can now be compared with a second ICU dataset using conservative outcome-proxy framing.

command_center_implication.primary_point: The command center should emphasize queue rank, priority tier, primary driver, trend direction, and lead-time context instead of raw score alone.
command_center_implication.pilot_safe_note: Raw score saturation should be interpreted as a reason to show richer prioritization context, not as a standalone clinical conclusion.

score_saturation_queue_audit.pilot_safe_note: Score saturation is handled through queue rank, priority tier, primary driver, trend direction, and lead-time context.

subcohort_validation_b.public_policy: Aggregate metrics only; row-level MIMIC-derived subset remains local-only.

interpretation_guidance.main_question: Does the ERA threshold story remain directionally consistent on a different deterministic subcohort?

comparison.directional_pattern: t=4.0 should be interpreted as higher-detection/high-acuity; t=6.0 as conservative low-burden review queue.

interpretation_guidance.caution: This is retrospective subcohort testing, not prospective validation.

subcohort_validation_b.pilot_safe_note: Decision support only. Retrospective analysis only.

interpretation_guidance.main_question: Does the ERA threshold story remain directionally consistent on a second different deterministic subcohort?

comparison.directional_pattern: t=4.0 should be interpreted as higher-detection/high-acuity; t=6.0 as conservative low-burden review queue.

interpretation_guidance.caution: This is retrospective subcohort testing, not prospective validation.

cross_cohort_validation_comparison.public_policy: Aggregate metrics only. No row-level MIMIC-derived data, no real identifiers, no exact case-linked timestamps.
cross_cohort_validation_comparison.pilot_safe_note: Decision support only. Retrospective aggregate validation only.

stable_cross_cohort_validation_release.cross_cohort_headline: Across the full validation cohort and two deterministic patient-level subcohorts (577–1,705 cases each), the conservative t=6.0 ERA setting showed consistent low-burden review-queue performance, with 94.0%–94.9% alert reduction, 3.7%–4.4% ERA FPR, 14.1%–16.0% event-cluster detection, and a stable 4.0 hours median lead-time context across all cohorts.
stable_cross_cohort_validation_release.public_policy: Aggregate DUA-safe evidence only. Row-level MIMIC-derived data remains local-only.
stable_cross_cohort_validation_release.pilot_safe_note: Decision support only. Retrospective aggregate analysis only.

eicu_outcome_proxy_validation.event_definition: Outcome-proxy event labels from mortality/discharge status fields using relative ICU offset timing.
eicu_outcome_proxy_validation.public_policy: Aggregate metrics only; raw eICU files and row-level ERA-format cohort remain local-only.
